title: Qualifying Impeding
description: Driver on fast lap blocked by slow car in Turn 7-8 complex
explanation: Driver CAR-14 was on a slow lap and failed to move off the racing line when CAR-01 approached on a fast lap. The speed differential of 45 km/h and the location in a high-speed section constitute clear impeding under Article 37.5.

precedent_cases.description: Impeding on fast lap in high-speed section

alternative_interpretations: Could be considered racing incident if CAR-14 was also on fast lap
Severity could be reduced if visibility was compromised
fan_mode_summary: The slower car didn't get out of the way when the faster car was coming up behind on a flying lap. That's against the rules and usually gets a penalty.

title: Unsafe Pit Release
description: Car released into path of approaching car with insufficient gap
explanation: Team released CAR-07 with only 0.8 seconds gap to approaching CAR-23. This falls below the minimum safe release threshold and forced CAR-23 to take evasive action.
alternative_interpretations: Could argue CAR-23 had opportunity to avoid
Gap measurement accuracy could be disputed
fan_mode_summary: The team let their car out of the pit box when another car was too close. That's dangerous and gets penalized.

evidence.description: Pit lane camera shows unsafe release

title: Lap 1 Avoidable Collision
description: Contact at Turn 1 causing retirement of one car
explanation: CAR-11 made contact with CAR-18 at Turn 1 entry. Analysis shows CAR-11 had 70% fault due to late braking and insufficient space left. However, racing incident factors reduce certainty.
alternative_interpretations: Could be considered racing incident due to Lap 1 circumstances
CAR-18 could have left more space
fan_mode_summary: Two cars touched at the first corner on the opening lap. One driver was mostly at fault for not leaving enough space, so they got a penalty.

title: Track Limits & Gained Advantage
description: Multiple track limit violations with position advantage gained
explanation: CAR-05 exceeded track limits 4 times in 3 laps, gaining approximately 0.3 seconds advantage. This exceeds the tolerance threshold and resulted in position gain over CAR-09.
alternative_interpretations: Some violations may have been forced by other cars
fan_mode_summary: The driver went off track too many times and gained an unfair advantage, so they have to give back the position they gained.

title: Rain Strategy Dilemma
description: Rain approaching, optimal pit window decision required
explanation: Weather radar shows rain approaching in 8-10 minutes. Current tire age (18 laps) and degradation rate suggest pitting within 2 laps provides optimal window before rain. Extending risks being caught out by weather change.

alternatives.reasoning: Immediate pit ensures dry tire change but loses track position
High risk of being caught by rain on worn tires

suggested_decision: Pit within 2 laps for fresh intermediates
fan_mode_summary: Rain is coming soon, and the tires are getting old. The team should pit in the next lap or two to get fresh tires before the rain hits.
